name: TCL 65Q650G Q6 QLED 4K Smart TV, 65-inch (2024)

specs.Panel: QLED with Mini-LED backlight (100+ dimming zones)

whatWorks: The Mini-LED backlight with 100+ dimming zones delivers black levels and contrast that look genuinely premium — HDR content lights up with real pop and deep shadows that sub-$1000 LCD TVs typically can't match. Dolby Vision IQ adjusts tone mapping based on ambient light, so daytime viewing doesn't wash out.

Four HDMI 2.1 ports is a spec most $1500+ TVs offer; getting all four at $799 CAD is exceptional. Combined with 120Hz VRR and ALLM, this is a legitimately capable gaming TV for PS5 and Xbox Series X owners who don't want to spend $1500+ on a Sony or LG.

Google TV is one of the best smart TV platforms available — universal search across Netflix, Disney+, Crave, and Prime Video in one bar, Chromecast built in, and Google Assistant for voice control. No need to buy a separate streaming stick.

TCL's Q6 is the Canadian branding for the 6-Series, which has been the go-to Wirecutter recommendation for value-focused buyers for years. The 2024 version adds Mini-LED upgrades that meaningfully narrow the gap with panel tech costing twice as much.
worthKnowing: Local dimming, while improved, still produces occasional blooming around bright objects on dark backgrounds — a white subtitle over a night scene will glow faintly. It's manageable but visible if you're watching in a dark room and comparing against OLED.

Built-in speakers are adequate but not great. Dialogue is clear, but bass is thin and surround staging is flat. For anyone with a soundbar or receiver, this isn't an issue — but buyers who rely on TV speakers for their main audio will notice the limitation.

Google TV's ad-forward home screen surfaces promoted content and recommendations before your own apps. You can customize it but can't fully disable promoted rows — some people find this intrusive on a TV they've already paid for.
The TCL remote is functional but not backlit, which is a minor but real annoyance in dark rooms. Competing remotes from LG and Samsung include backlighting as standard at lower price points.

commonComplaints.title: Blooming around bright objects…
commonComplaints.body: Blooming around subtitles and bright logos on dark backgrounds is visible if you sit close. Not a dealbreaker but noticeable in a dark room.

commonComplaints.title: Built-in speakers are thin…
commonComplaints.body: Sound is fine for casual TV but the bass is weak and voices sound a bit hollow at higher volumes. I added a soundbar after a week.

commonComplaints.title: Google TV home screen is ad-heavy…
commonComplaints.body: The home screen pushes promoted content constantly. You can rearrange apps but the ad rows never fully go away.

commonComplaints.title: Remote has no backlight…
commonComplaints.body: Watching in the dark and hunting for the mute button every time. Samsung and LG remotes have backlights — TCL should too at this price.

verdict: The TCL Q6 65-inch is the strongest value proposition in the $799 CAD TV market — Mini-LED backlighting, 120Hz with HDMI 2.1 for gaming, Dolby Vision IQ, and Google TV in one package. Blooming and mediocre speakers are real trade-offs, but neither undermines the picture quality advantage over IPS and VA competitors at similar prices. If your budget is under $900 and you want the best possible picture, this is the one to buy.

realBuyers.quote: I came from a $1200 Sony from 2019 and honestly this picture is better. The Mini-LED makes HDR content look stunning.
Gaming on PS5 with 120Hz and no input lag is incredible. Four HDMI 2.1 ports at this price is unreal.
Google TV finds what I want to watch faster than any other smart TV I've owned. Netflix, Disney+, Crave — one search bar.
Best TV I've ever owned. The picture in dark room HDR mode blew me away. Can't believe this is $800.

metaDescription: TCL Q6 65-inch review for Canada: Mini-LED, 120Hz, HDMI 2.1, Dolby Vision IQ at $799 CAD. Honest on blooming and speaker limits.
introText: The TCL Q6 65-inch (ASIN B0CTXY8NPN) is the Canadian version of TCL's 6-Series — a Mini-LED QLED TV with 120Hz, four HDMI 2.1 ports, and Google TV at around $799 CAD on Amazon.ca.
